target.title: fix(update): continue after package doctor warnings
target.body:
## Summary

- Downgrade package post-install `openclaw doctor --fix` only for the dedicated exit code, a normally terminated child, and a valid typed deferred-configured-plugin-repair advisory after package verification.
- Keep unknown nonzero exits, killed/timed-out/signaled doctor runs, malformed or missing results, and the non-converging generic update path blocking.
- Surface advisory doctor diagnostics in progress and summaries, then continue through mandatory post-core plugin convergence before restart.
- Scope advisory details to repairs explicitly deferred by doctor so successful unrelated changes, including stale bundled-record cleanup, do not produce false exit-86 warnings.
- Place the advisory sidecar under OpenClaw's secured preferred temp root.

## Verification

- Focused maintainer proof on head `a6922c388b254290d3b1c51628d53c8731d23a18`: 249 tests across three Vitest shards.
- Scoped `oxfmt`, `oxlint`, and `git diff --check` passed.
- Fresh Codex autoreview passed in local patch mode and full branch mode against current `origin/main`.
- AWS Crabbox packed-package E2E passed: run `run_73443414fbed`, lease `cbx_1ef151cd24be`. The current head updated through the real package CLI path, accepted only the typed exit-86 advisory, and completed post-core plugin convergence with status `ok`.
- The contributor's broader 355-test proof and `pnpm check` remain valid for the original branch implementation.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: The `openclaw update` CLI package path continues past post-install doctor only for the dedicated exit code, a normally terminated child, and a valid typed deferred-configured-plugin-repair advisory. The advisory is now emitted only when the repair producer explicitly reports deferred repair details; unrelated successful doctor changes do not become advisory exits. The package updater then runs mandatory post-core plugin convergence before restart. The non-converging generic `runGatewayUpdate` path keeps post-install doctor nonzero results blocking and does not participate in the advisory IPC contract.

Real environment tested: Current head `a6922c388b254290d3b1c51628d53c8731d23a18` was tested locally on macOS and in AWS Crabbox Linux with Node 24. The packed current-head tarball was installed as the updater parent, then updated through the same packed candidate so both sides of the cross-version IPC path used the reviewed code.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MANAGED_ENV_KEYS -u OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_SERVICE_PID -u OPENCLAW_LAUNCHD_LABEL -u OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT -u OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_NAME -u OPENCLAW_WRAPPER PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr-91586-vitest-final node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/update-runner.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts
pnpm check
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
git merge-tree --write-tree upstream/main HEAD
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=kepler-alpha --thinking codex=high
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=kepler-alpha --thinking codex=high
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed
```

Evidence after fix: The maintainer-focused suite passed 249 tests covering the IPC parser/path contract, package update advisory classifier, CLI continuation path, configured-plugin repair producer, and release repair flow. Scoped formatting/lint and `git diff --check` passed. Fresh local and full-branch Codex autoreviews reported no accepted/actionable findings. AWS Crabbox run `run_73443414fbed` completed the packed package update with overall status `ok`, doctor exit code `86`, advisory kind `package-post-install-doctor`, and post-core plugin status `ok`.

Copied live output excerpt from current-head AWS Crabbox run `run_73443414fbed`:

```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "mode": "npm",
  "doctorExitCode": 86,
  "doctorAdvisory": "package-post-install-doctor",
  "postUpdatePluginsStatus": "ok"
}
```

Observed result after fix: The typed advisory gate authorizes only the intended recoverable doctor result in the CLI package updater, and the advisory producer now distinguishes actual deferred repair details from unrelated successful repair changes. Unknown exits, missing or malformed results, killed runs, signals, and timeouts remain blocking. The sidecar path uses OpenClaw's secured preferred temp root and passes the current temp-path policy guard.

What was not tested: The exact advisory E2E used `--no-restart`; managed gateway restart was not exercised. Azure capacity timed out after ten minutes, so the successful current-head proof used brokered AWS Crabbox. No live or protected channel was touched.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
